portfolio_rationale: LLY stands apart with 54% revenue growth, bullish technical signal, price above moving averages, and positive momentum — the only ticker with a clean multi-factor bull case. AAPL and GOOGL offer strong fundamentals with manageable bear cases, while AMZN and BRK.B have more technical headwinds. XOM is excluded due to a bearish technical signal, negative momentum on both 5-day and 20-day timeframes, and negative news sentiment, making its bull case the least convincing.

LLY.reason: Exceptional revenue growth (54%), bullish technical signal with price above MAs and positive momentum on both timeframes, and the strongest fundamental profile in the universe with no material bear case.

AAPL.reason: Strong FCF, 22% revenue growth, and high margins with an RSI near 30 suggesting oversold conditions; bear case liquidity concerns are immaterial given cash generation.

GOOGL.reason: Strong ROE (36%), adequate liquidity, positive 20-day momentum, and well-rebutted bear case, though slightly weaker technical picture than AAPL.

AMZN.reason: Solid fundamentals and positive news sentiment, but recurring technical weakness (20-day below 50-day MA, EMA crossover negative) warrants a smaller position.

BRK.B.reason: Solid profitability metrics but negative momentum on both timeframes, minimal revenue growth, and neutral news trends make this the weakest bullish case among held positions.

XOM.reason: Excluded due to bearish technical analyst signal, negative 5-day and 20-day return momentum, negative news sentiment, and the weakest fundamental bull case in the universe.
